changes_made: Changed k8s_resource_quota_configured to k8s_namespace_resource_quota_configured for clarity on resource scope
Changed k8s_horizontal_pod_autoscaler_enabled to k8s_pod_horizontal_autoscaler_enabled for consistency with naming conventions
review_notes: The functions are relevant to Kubernetes capacity planning and contingency planning. Resource quotas, autoscalers, and disruption budgets are appropriate mechanisms for ensuring capacity and availability. Naming adjustments were made for clarity and consistency with Kubernetes terminology.
naming_improvements: Ensure resource scope is clear in function names, such as specifying 'namespace' for resource quotas.
Maintain consistency in naming by aligning with existing patterns, such as using 'pod_horizontal_autoscaler' instead of 'horizontal_pod_autoscaler'.
title: Contingency Plan | Capacity Planning
step1_reasoning: The FedRAMP CP-2 (2) control focuses on contingency planning and capacity planning, which in Kubernetes can be addressed through resource management and scaling mechanisms. Resource quotas ensure that namespaces do not exceed their allocated resources, preventing resource exhaustion. Horizontal Pod Autoscalers and Cluster Autoscalers help manage workloads by automatically adjusting the number of pods or nodes based on demand, ensuring capacity is available. Pod Disruption Budgets help maintain application availability during voluntary disruptions, contributing to contingency planning.
